actions.name: Create Jira ticket
actions.description: Creates a Jira ticket for newly opened GitHub issues, syncing relevant details such as the issue title and number. It utilizes a GitHub Action to transfer information from GitHub to Jira, ensuring that issues are tracked in the project management system.

actions.name: Sync board list
actions.description: Updates the board JSON in the armbian/armbian.github.io repository based on changes to board configuration files. Sends a repository dispatch event to trigger further processing related to generating a build engine inventory.
actions.execution_method: push to main branch with changes in config/boards/*.*

actions.name: Sync labels
actions.description: Syncs labels from a YAML file to issues and pull requests based on modifications. Utilizes a labeler action to apply labels defined in '.github/labels.yml' when changes are detected or manually triggered. Supports dry-run mode for pull requests to preview label changes without applying them.
actions.execution_method: workflow_dispatch, push to main branch affecting .github/labels.yml, pull_request affecting .github/labels.yml

actions.name: Sync maintainers
actions.description: Syncs maintainers from a contacts database to board configuration files, updating the BOARD_MAINTAINER property. If changes are detected, it creates a Pull Request to reflect these updates. Additionally, it handles the renaming of files for boards without maintainers and regenerates the CODEOWNERS file.

actions.name: Sync tools
actions.description: Updates tool versions in scripts by fetching the latest releases from specified GitHub repositories. Creates a pull request only if the version in the script is actually updated, allowing for easy management of dependencies that cannot be handled by dependabot. New tools can be added to the update process by modifying a matrix configuration.
actions.cron_schedule: at 42 minutes past the hour, at 3:00, on day 16

actions.name: Dependency Review
actions.description: Scans dependency manifest files in pull requests to identify known-vulnerable package versions. Blocks merging of PRs that introduce these vulnerable packages if the workflow run is marked as required. Enhances security by auditing outbound calls during the process.

actions.name: Dispatch to fork
actions.description: Dispatches events to a forked repository based on various GitHub events like push, release, deployment, and pull requests. Utilizes a secret token to trigger a repository dispatch with details about the event, including the reference and SHA. Executes only on forked repositories to facilitate communication with the original repository.

actions.name: Mirror to Codeberg
actions.description: Pushes the contents of the repository to the Codeberg mirror, ensuring that the latest changes are reflected in the mirrored repository. It includes a safety check to prevent execution from forks and cancels older runs if a new one starts.

actions.name: Announce merge
actions.description: Announces merges to the main branch by sending a message to Discord with details about the merge, including the repository, actor, and commit message.
actions.execution_method: Triggered on push to main branch

actions.name: Announce PR
actions.description: Announces pull requests labeled with a specific ID on Discord for review, providing details about the pull request and its author. It retrieves the repository content and sends a formatted message to a specified Discord webhook.

actions.name: Auto-label PR
actions.description: Automatically labels pull requests based on size, file categories, and creation date, while also removing the 'Ready to merge' label when applicable. It categorizes PRs by analyzing the number of changed lines, matching files against a configuration, and assigning quarterly labels based on the PR creation month. This ensures that PRs are appropriately tagged for easier management and tracking.

actions.name: Build artifacts
actions.description: Generates artifacts for pull requests labeled with 'Build' by authorized members of the 'Release manager' team. It checks the user's team membership and then compiles the artifacts if the user is authorized. The process includes a concurrency control to manage builds for the same pull request.

actions.name: Check board assets
actions.description: Verifies assets for newly added board configurations in pull requests, ensuring that required board images and vendor logos exist in the designated website repository. It checks for new configuration files, validates the presence of associated assets, and comments on the pull request if any assets are missing.

actions.name: Analyze kernel security
actions.description: Analyzes kernel security options by checking the Linux kernel configuration files against security hardening standards. It audits outbound calls, retrieves changed files, and runs a kernel hardening checker on the relevant configuration files, excluding unsupported RISC-V configs. Results are formatted for display in the GitHub Actions job summary.

actions.name: Clean workflow logs
actions.description: Cleans up workflow logs by deleting old runs based on specified criteria. It removes logs that are older than a defined number of days, which can be set via input or defaults to 10 days. The process helps maintain a tidy repository by managing log storage effectively.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on Mon

actions.name: Label PR on approval
actions.description: Labels pull requests as 'Ready to merge' upon approval and removes review-related labels. It downloads the pull request number from a previous workflow run and uses it to manage labels based on approval status. Additionally, it provides feedback through comments on the pull request.

actions.name: Lint scripts (post)
actions.description: Downloads diagnostic artifacts from a previous linting job and posts the findings as inline review comments and suggestion blocks on pull requests using reviewdog. It ensures that diagnostics are surfaced even when the initial linting fails, without checking out potentially untrusted code. This workflow operates in the context of the BASE repository to maintain security while providing feedback on code quality.

actions.name: Lint scripts
actions.description: Lints shell scripts in pull requests using ShellCheck, providing a pass/fail status and generating artifacts for review comments and auto-fix suggestions. It checks for critical issues and produces findings in a format consumable by reviewdog for inline comments. The workflow ensures diagnostics are available even if the linting fails.

actions.name: Listen PR review
actions.description: Saves the pull request number when a review is approved. It uploads the PR number as an artifact for further use. This helps in tracking and managing pull request reviews effectively.

actions.name: Rewrite kernel configs
actions.description: Builds and rewrites kernel configurations across multiple boards and families by generating a dynamic matrix of configurations, executing a rewrite process, and aggregating changes into a single pull request. It collects modified files and summarizes the changes, ensuring only relevant updates are included. The workflow also manages artifact uploads and PR creation for the aggregated changes.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on MON

actions.name: Rewrite patches
actions.description: Rewrites kernel or U-Boot patches for a specified board and branch, updating the repository and checking for changes. If changes are detected, it creates a pull request with a summary of the modifications. The process includes cloning the repository, running a compilation script, and generating a detailed report of file changes.

actions.name: Security scan
actions.description: Runs a security scan using Scorecards to analyze the codebase for vulnerabilities and best practices. Publishes results to the code-scanning dashboard and optionally uploads the results as artifacts. This analysis is restricted to the default branch and is performed weekly or on specific push events.
actions.cron_schedule: at 30 minutes past the hour, at 1:00, on Sat

actions.name: Validate board configs
actions.description: Validates changed board configuration files in pull requests by running a Python script that checks for errors and emits annotations on the PR diff. It only processes files that have been modified, ensuring efficient validation without unnecessary checks. The workflow blocks merging if any errors are found during validation.

actions.name: Watchdog
actions.description: Monitors and reruns failed jobs for specified workflows, ensuring stability in CI/CD processes. It checks the latest run status and triggers a rerun if the previous attempt failed and hasn't exceeded the maximum retry limit. This helps maintain the reliability of automated workflows.

actions.name: Welcome first-time issue contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time issue contributors by sending a personalized message and labeling the issue as 'Good first issue'. It enhances community engagement and encourages collaboration by providing contact options for updates.

actions.name: Welcome first-time PR contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time pull request contributors by sending a personalized message when they open a pull request. The message expresses gratitude for their contribution and encourages further engagement with the project community.
